devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । पौरोहित्याय भगवान्वृतः काव्यः किलासुरैः । षण्डामर्कौ सुतौ तस्य दैत्यराजगृहान्तिके ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācapaurohityāya bhagavānvṛtaḥ kāvyaḥ kilāsuraiḥṣaṇḍāmarkau sutau tasya daityarājagṛhāntike
translation:
Nārada said:
As is reported, the glorious Kāvya (Śukra) was selected by demons to fill the office of the royal priest. His two sons, Śaṇḍa and Amarka, stayed near the palace of the king of Daityas.

devanagari: तौ राज्ञा प्रापितं बालं प्रह्लादं नयकोविदम् । पाठयामासतुः पाठ्यानन्यांश्चासुरबालकान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tau rājñā prāpitaṃ bālaṃ prahlādaṃ nayakovidampāṭhayāmāsatuḥ pāṭhyānanyāṃścāsurabālakān
translation: They taught the course of studies (in politics and such other sciences) to Prahlāda and other eligible Asura students sent to them by the king, even though Prahlāda was expert in philosophy.

devanagari: यत्तत्र गुरुणा प्रोक्तं शुश्रुवेऽनुपपाठ च । न साधु मनसा मेने स्वपरासद्ग्रहाश्रयम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yattatra guruṇā proktaṃ śuśruve'nupapāṭha cana sādhu manasā mene svaparāsadgrahāśrayam
translation: Whatever was taught there by the teacher, he (carefully) listened and reproduced it. But he did not approve of the teaching that was on the false notion of distinguishing between (man and man) as friends and enemies.

devanagari: एकदासुरराट्पुत्रमङ्कमारोप्य पाण्डव । पप्रच्छ कथ्यतां वत्स मन्यते साधु यद्भवान् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadāsurarāṭputramaṅkamāropya pāṇḍavapapraccha kathyatāṃ vatsa manyate sādhu yadbhavān
translation: Oh Pāṇḍava, once upon a time, the king of Asuras seated the child on his lap and asked him, “What do you regard as good, my child?”

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्लाद उवाच । तत्साधु मन्येऽसुरवर्य देहिनां सदा समुद्विग्नधियामसद्ग्रहात् । हित्वात्मपातं गृहमन्धकूपं वनं गतो यद्धरिमाश्रयेत ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprahlāda uvācatatsādhu manye'suravarya dehināṃ sadā samudvignadhiyāmasadgrahāthitvātmapātaṃ gṛhamandhakūpaṃ vanaṃ gato yaddharimāśrayeta
translation:
Prahlāda replied:
Oh Prominent Asura! To embodied beings who are always mentally harassed with the false notion of ‘I’ and ‘Mine’, I regard that it is in their interest that they should abandon the hidden-well-like household which is degrading to the Soul and should enter into the woods and seek shelter with Hari.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । श्रुत्वा पुत्रगिरो दैत्यः परपक्षसमाहिताः । जहास बुद्धिर्बालानां भिद्यते परबुद्धिभिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaśrutvā putragiro daityaḥ parapakṣasamāhitāḥjahāsa buddhirbālānāṃ bhidyate parabuddhibhiḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Having heard the words of his son which were in favour of the hostile party, he laughed (derisively) and exclaimed: “How the understanding of children gets perverted through contact with others (inimical element)!”

devanagari: सम्यग्विधार्यतां बालो गुरुगेहे द्विजातिभिः । विष्णुपक्षैः प्रतिच्छन्नैर्न भिद्येतास्य धीर्यथा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: samyagvidhāryatāṃ bālo gurugehe dvijātibhiḥviṣṇupakṣaiḥ praticchannairna bhidyetāsya dhīryathā
translation: Let the child be so carefully guarded in his preceptor’s house, that this intellect should not be vitiated by Brāhmaṇas who are the partisans of Viṣṇu in cognito.

devanagari: गृहमानीतमाहूय प्रह्रादं दैत्ययाजकाः । प्रशस्य श्लक्ष्णया वाचा समपृच्छन्त सामभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhamānītamāhūya prahrādaṃ daityayājakāḥpraśasya ślakṣṇayā vācā samapṛcchanta sāmabhiḥ
translation: When brought home (from the palace of Hiraṇyakaśipu), the royal priests of Daityas sent for him. Praising him in soft words, they enquired of him in coaxing terms and in a conciliatory tone.

devanagari: वत्स प्रह्राद भद्रं ते सत्यं कथय मा मृषा । बालानति कुतस्तुभ्यमेष बुद्धिविपर्ययः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vatsa prahrāda bhadraṃ te satyaṃ kathaya mā mṛṣābālānati kutastubhyameṣa buddhiviparyayaḥ
translation: “Oh child Prahlāda! May you be happy! Please tell (us) the truth; do not tell a lie. What caused this perversion of intellect which is beyonḍ(i.e. not found in) boys of your age?

devanagari: बुद्धिभेदः परकृत उताहो ते स्वतोऽभवत् । भण्यतां श्रोतुकामानां गुरूणां कुलनन्दन ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: buddhibhedaḥ parakṛta utāho te svato'bhavatbhaṇyatāṃ śrotukāmānāṃ gurūṇāṃ kulanandana
translation: Is this change (distraction) in your mind brought out by others? Or has it taken place automatically due to your innate nature?

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्राद उवाच । परः स्वश्चेत्यसद्ग्राहः पुंसां यन्मायया कृतः । विमोहितधियां दृष्टस्तस्मै भगवते नमः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprahrāda uvācaparaḥ svaścetyasadgrāhaḥ puṃsāṃ yanmāyayā kṛtaḥvimohitadhiyāṃ dṛṣṭastasmai bhagavate namaḥ
translation:
Prahlāda said:
Obeisance to the glorious Lord whose deluding power (Māyā) has created the wrong concept that ‘this is mine’ and ‘this is another’s’, found in men whose intellect is deluded by Māyā.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī attributes this delusion to the Lord.
“vimohitetyanenā pīśvara-kartṛtvaṃ jñāyate /”

devanagari: स यदानुव्रतः पुंसां पशुबुद्धिर्विभिद्यते । अन्य एष तथान्योऽहमिति भेदगतासती ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yadānuvrataḥ puṃsāṃ paśubuddhirvibhidyateanya eṣa tathānyo'hamiti bhedagatāsatī
translation: When He is favourable, the beast-like understanding apprehending differentiation that “This is different (from me) and I am different (from him)”, comes to an end.

devanagari: स एष आत्मा स्वपरेत्यबुद्धिभिर्दुरत्ययानुक्रमणो निरूप्यते । मुह्यन्ति यद्वर्त्मनि वेदवादिनो ब्रह्मादयो ह्येष भिनत्ति मे मतिम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa ātmā svaparetyabuddhibhirduratyayānukramaṇo nirūpyatemuhyanti yadvartmani vedavādino brahmādayo hyeṣa bhinatti me matim
translation: It is the Supreme Soul which is looked upon as one’s own Self and another, by stupid people to whom His ways are inscrutable and indescribable. (It is however no wonder.) Even god Brahmā and other exponents of the Veda, are stupefied when they enquire about his nature. It is that Soul who has changed my mind (and outlook).

devanagari: यथा भ्राम्यत्ययो ब्रह्मन्स्वयमाकर्षसन्निधौ । तथा मे भिद्यते चेतश्चक्रपाणेर्यदृच्छया ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā bhrāmyatyayo brahmansvayamākarṣasannidhautathā me bhidyate cetaścakrapāṇeryadṛcchayā
translation: Oh Brahman! Just as a piece of iron within the vicinity of a magnet is automatically attracted towards it, my mind, of its own accord (without any ostensible reason), is gravitated to Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । एतावद्ब्राह्मणायोक्त्वा विरराम महामतिः । तं सन्निभर्त्स्य कुपितः सुदीनो राजसेवकः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaetāvadbrāhmaṇāyoktvā virarāma mahāmatiḥtaṃ sannibhartsya kupitaḥ sudīno rājasevakaḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Having spoken (explained) as above, the highly intelligent Prahlāda stopped (speaking). Reproaching him threateningly, the wretched royal servant got enraged (and ordered):

devanagari: आनीयतामरे वेत्रमस्माकमयशस्करः । कुलाङ्गारस्य दुर्बुद्धेश्चतुर्थोऽस्योदितो दमः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ānīyatāmare vetramasmākamayaśaskaraḥkulāṅgārasya durbuddheścaturtho'syodito damaḥ
translation: “Who is there? Bring me a cane. This boy brings disreputation to us. The fourth expedient (corporal punishment) has been prescribed in the Śāstras, in case of such a wicked-minded fellow who ruins his family.

devanagari: दैतेयचन्दनवने जातोऽयं कण्टकद्रुमः । यन्मूलोन्मूलपरशोर्विष्णोर्नालायितोऽर्भकः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: daiteyacandanavane jāto'yaṃ kaṇṭakadrumaḥyanmūlonmūlaparaśorviṣṇornālāyito'rbhakaḥ
translation: This boy is like a thorny tree grown in the forest of sandal trees in the form of Daityas. He serves as a handle to Viṣṇu who is (like) an axe cutting the roots of that forest.”

devanagari: इति तं विविधोपायैर्भीषयंस्तर्जनादिभिः । प्रह्रादं ग्राहयामास त्रिवर्गस्योपपादनम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti taṃ vividhopāyairbhīṣayaṃstarjanādibhiḥprahrādaṃ grāhayāmāsa trivargasyopapādanam
translation: Intimidating him with various expedients like threats (corporal punishment and others), they made Prahlāda to learn (the text dealing with) the first three objects of human life (viz. dharma, artha and kāma).

devanagari: तत एनं गुरुर्ज्ञात्वा ज्ञातज्ञेयचतुष्टयम् । दैत्येन्द्रं दर्शयामास मातृमृष्टमलङ्कृतम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tata enaṃ gururjñātvā jñātajñeyacatuṣṭayamdaityendraṃ darśayāmāsa mātṛmṛṣṭamalaṅkṛtam
translation: Later on, the teacher was convinced that Prahlāda had mastered (known) the four branches in political strategy (viz. sāma, dāna, daṇḍa and bheda). After Prahlāda’s mother bathed, dressed and adorned him, he was brought to the presence of the king of Daityas.

devanagari: पादयोः पतितं बालं प्रतिनन्द्याशिषासुरः । परिष्वज्य चिरं दोर्भ्यां परमामाप निर्वृतिम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: pādayoḥ patitaṃ bālaṃ pratinandyāśiṣāsuraḥpariṣvajya ciraṃ dorbhyāṃ paramāmāpa nirvṛtim
translation: The Asura (Hiraṇyakaśipu) greeted with blessings his child who lay prostrate at his feet. For a long time he hugged him in his arms and felt deep satisfaction and happiness.

devanagari: आरोप्याङ्कमवघ्राय मूर्धन्यश्रुकलाम्बुभिः । आसिञ्चन्विकसद्वक्त्रमिदमाह युधिष्ठिर ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: āropyāṅkamavaghrāya mūrdhanyaśrukalāmbubhiḥāsiñcanvikasadvaktramidamāha yudhiṣṭhira
translation: Placing him on the lap and smelling the crown of his head, he bathed him with tears. Oh Yudhiṣṭhira, then he spoke to the child of cheerful countenance.

devanagari: हिरण्यकशिपुरुवाच । प्रह्रादानूच्यतां तात स्वधीतं किञ्चिदुत्तमम् । कालेनैतावतायुष्मन्यदशिक्षद्गुरोर्भवान् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyakaśipuruvācaprahrādānūcyatāṃ tāta svadhītaṃ kiñciduttamamkālenaitāvatāyuṣmanyadaśikṣadgurorbhavān
translation:
Hiraṇyakaśipu said:
“My dear child Prahlāda! Please recite some excellent passages that you have learnt well from what your teacher has taught you up to this time, Oh long-lived one!”

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्राद उवाच । श्रवणं कीर्तनं विष्णोः स्मरणं पादसेवनम् । अर्चनं वन्दनं दास्यं सख्यमात्मनिवेदनम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīprahrāda uvācaśravaṇaṃ kīrtanaṃ viṣṇoḥ smaraṇaṃ pādasevanamarcanaṃ vandanaṃ dāsyaṃ sakhyamātmanivedanam
translation:
Prahlāda said:
“There are nine forms of devotion to Lord Viṣṇu—(1) to hear the names, episodes etc. of Viṣṇu, (2) to sing of his name and glories, (3) to Remember him (his name), (4) to render service unto him, (5) to worship him, (6) to pay obeisance to him, (7) to dedicate all one’s actions to him, (8) to confide to him as a friend, (9) to offer one’s body and belongings to his service and care. I regard it as the highest type of learning, if one offers himself completely to the Lord and performs this nine-fold devotion[2] (regards complete dedication as the condition precedent of real devotion)
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Kramasandarbha. has given a long but excellent exposition of Bhakti and its nine forms which deserve perusal in the original.

devanagari: इति पुंसार्पिता विष्णौ भक्तिश्चेन्नवलक्षणा । क्रियेत भगवत्यद्धा तन्मन्येऽधीतमुत्तमम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti puṃsārpitā viṣṇau bhaktiścennavalakṣaṇākriyeta bhagavatyaddhā tanmanye'dhītamuttamam
translation:
Prahlāda said:
“There are nine forms of devotion to Lord Viṣṇu—(1) to hear the names, episodes etc. of Viṣṇu, (2) to sing of his name and glories, (3) to Remember him (his name), (4) to render service unto him, (5) to worship him, (6) to pay obeisance to him, (7) to dedicate all one’s actions to him, (8) to confide to him as a friend, (9) to offer one’s body and belongings to his service and care. I regard it as the highest type of learning, if one offers himself completely to the Lord and performs this nine-fold devotion[2] (regards complete dedication as the condition precedent of real devotion)
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Kramasandarbha. has given a long but excellent exposition of Bhakti and its nine forms which deserve perusal in the original.

devanagari: निशम्यैतत्सुतवचो हिरण्यकशिपुस्तदा । गुरुपुत्रमुवाचेदं रुषा प्रस्फुरिताधरः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamyaitatsutavaco hiraṇyakaśipustadāguruputramuvācedaṃ ruṣā prasphuritādharaḥ
translation: Hearing these words of his son, Hiraṇyakaśipu, with his lips quivering with rage, reproached Śukra’s son (the preceptor’s son) as follows:

devanagari: ब्रह्मबन्धो किमेतत्ते विपक्षं श्रयतासता । असारं ग्राहितो बालो मामनादृत्य दुर्मते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmabandho kimetatte vipakṣaṃ śrayatāsatāasāraṃ grāhito bālo māmanādṛtya durmate
translation: “You wretch of a Brāhmaṇa! What does this mean? Wicked fellow! You sided with the enemies and disregarding me completely, you have taught some non-sense to the child.

devanagari: सन्ति ह्यसाधवो लोके दुर्मैत्राश्छद्मवेषिणः । तेषामुदेत्यघं काले रोगः पातकिनामिव ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: santi hyasādhavo loke durmaitrāśchadmaveṣiṇaḥteṣāmudetyaghaṃ kāle rogaḥ pātakināmiva
translation: There are wicked persons in this world who assume a friend’s garb, but their friendship is treacherous. Their sin (sinfulness) becomes manifest in due time, like the grave diseases of sinners.”[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a smṛti text according to which certain types of sinners, e.g., murderer of a Brāhmaṇa, a habitual drunkard, a debaucherous person violating his teacher’s bed have to suffer from heinous diseases even after completing their term in hell. The question is repeated by VC., Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa, Bālaprabodhini and others.

devanagari: श्रीगुरुपुत्र उवाच । न मत्प्रणीतं न परप्रणीतं सुतो वदत्येष तवेन्द्र शत्रो । नैसर्गिकीयं मतिरस्य राजन्नियच्छ मन्युं कददाः स्म मा नः ॥ २८ ...
english_devnagari: śrīguruputra uvācana matpraṇītaṃ na parapraṇītaṃ suto vadatyeṣa tavendra śatronaisargikīyaṃ matirasya rājanniyaccha manyuṃ kadadāḥ sma mā naḥ
translation:
Preceptor’s (Śukra’s) son said:
What this son of yours speaks is not taught by me or by any person. Oh foe of Indra! It is his innate intelligence! Oh king! Please control your anger. Do not lay the blame on us.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । गुरुणैवं प्रतिप्रोक्तो भूय आहासुरः सुतम् । न चेद्गुरुमुखीयं ते कुतोऽभद्रा सती मतिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaguruṇaivaṃ pratiprokto bhūya āhāsuraḥ sutamna cedgurumukhīyaṃ te kuto'bhadrā satī matiḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
When he was thus replied by (Prahlāda’s) preceptor, the Asura (Hiraṇyakaśipu) again asked his son: “If this perverted understanding has not been caused by the teaching of your preceptor, whence has it come, you inauspicious boy?”

devanagari: श्रीप्रह्राद उवाच । मतिर्न कृष्णे परतः स्वतो वा मिथोऽभिपद्येत गृहव्रतानाम् । अदान्तगोभिर्विशतां तमिस्रं पुनः पुनश्चर्वितचर्वणानाम् ॥ ३० ...
english_devnagari: śrīprahrāda uvācamatirna kṛṣṇe parataḥ svato vā mitho'bhipadyeta gṛhavratānāmadāntagobhirviśatāṃ tamisraṃ punaḥ punaścarvitacarvaṇānām
translation:
Prahlāda said:
(Unswerving attachment and) firm fixation of the mind on Kṛṣṇa does not arise through (the advice of) another, or of one’s own accord, or by mutual efforts in the case of those who are firmly devoted to their household (as if it were a sacred vow). Nor (is it engendered) in persons who have not controlled their senses, and who now and again enter the darkness of Saṃsāra, and who again and again ruminate (repeatedly experience) what they have enjoyed (in former births).

devanagari: न ते विदुः स्वार्थगतिं हि विष्णुं दुराशया ये बहिरर्थमानिनः । अन्धा यथान्धैरुपनीयमानास्तेऽपीशतन्त्र्यामुरुदाम्नि बद्धाः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: na te viduḥ svārthagatiṃ hi viṣṇuṃ durāśayā ye bahirarthamāninaḥandhā yathāndhairupanīyamānāste'pīśatantryāmurudāmni baddhāḥ
translation: They whose heart is polluted (by their attachment to worldly objects) do not know Viṣṇu, to be their (real) objective in life and goal. For they look up for guidance to those who regard the external objects as their aim in life, and are like the blind persons led by the blind. They are tied down (like beasts) with Lord Viṣṇu’s long rope in the form of Vedic injunctions prescribing rites to be performed for future good. (These persons are like persons who go to the east in search of an object lost in the west).

devanagari: नैषां मतिस्तावदुरुक्रमाङ्घ्रिं स्पृशत्यनर्थापगमो यदर्थः । महीयसां पादरजोऽभिषेकं निष्किञ्चनानां न वृणीत यावत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: naiṣāṃ matistāvadurukramāṅghriṃ spṛśatyanarthāpagamo yadarthaḥmahīyasāṃ pādarajo'bhiṣekaṃ niṣkiñcanānāṃ na vṛṇīta yāvat
translation: So long as persons do not seek bath in the dust on the feet of those noble Souls (the devotees of Viṣṇu) who have shed off attachment to worldly objects, the mind of such persons cannot touch (reach) the feet of Lord Viṣṇu (the Lord of long strides as Trivikrama or the Lord of infinite prowess), the object of which is the cessation of Saṃsāra (the cycle of births and deaths and the miseries involved).”[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā states: Unless one secures the grace of such exalted Souls, the exact implication of Vedānta and the consequent liberation is impossible.
Anvitārthaprakāśikā. also agrees with this view.
Bālaprabodhini and Bhaktamanorañjanī state that hereby Prahlāda suggests that it was through Nārada’s grace that he got this spiritual revelation.

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वोपरतं पुत्रं हिरण्यकशिपू रुषा । अन्धीकृतात्मा स्वोत्सङ्गान्निरस्यत महीतले ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvoparataṃ putraṃ hiraṇyakaśipū ruṣāandhīkṛtātmā svotsaṅgānnirasyata mahītale
translation: Hardly did the son (Prahlāda) stop speaking, when Hiraṇyakaśipu with his mind blinded with rage, hurled him from his lap, down to the ground.

devanagari: आहामर्षरुषाविष्टः कषायीभूतलोचनः । वध्यतामाश्वयं वध्यो निःसारयत नैरृताः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: āhāmarṣaruṣāviṣṭaḥ kaṣāyībhūtalocanaḥvadhyatāmāśvayaṃ vadhyo niḥsārayata nairṛtāḥ
translation: Overwhelmed with unbeareble (violent) wrath, and with eyes reddened with anger, he ordered, “This boy deserves capital punishment. Let him be executed immediately. Oh Nairṛtas, take him away from my presence.

devanagari: अयं मे भ्रातृहा सोऽयं हित्वा स्वान्सुहृदोऽधमः । पितृव्यहन्तुः पादौ यो विष्णोर्दासवदर्चति ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ me bhrātṛhā so'yaṃ hitvā svānsuhṛdo'dhamaḥpitṛvyahantuḥ pādau yo viṣṇordāsavadarcati
translation: This (boy) is verily the murderer of my brother. For this wretched fellow, deserting his own well-wishers, worships like a slave the feet of Viṣṇu, the assassinator of his paternal uncle.

devanagari: विष्णोर्वा साध्वसौ किं नु करिष्यत्यसमञ्जसः । सौहृदं दुस्त्यजं पित्रोरहाद्यः पञ्चहायनः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇorvā sādhvasau kiṃ nu kariṣyatyasamañjasaḥsauhṛdaṃ dustyajaṃ pitrorahādyaḥ pañcahāyanaḥ
translation: Or even to Viṣṇu what good purpose can he serve, I doubt; for even as a child of five, he forfeited his parental affection which is so hard to sever.

devanagari: परोऽप्यपत्यं हितकृद्यथौषधं स्वदेहजोऽप्यामयवत्सुतोऽहितः । छिन्द्यात्तदङ्गं यदुतात्मनोऽहितं शेषं सुखं जीवति यद्विवर्जनात् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: paro'pyapatyaṃ hitakṛdyathauṣadhaṃ svadehajo'pyāmayavatsuto'hitaḥchindyāttadaṅgaṃ yadutātmano'hitaṃ śeṣaṃ sukhaṃ jīvati yadvivarjanāt
translation: If beneficial, even a stranger should be treated as a child, like medicine (though the medicinal herbs grow into the forest—outside and away from one’s home—they are preserved at home). If a son begotten by one’s own self becomes inimical (works against one’s interest) he should be treated like a disease. One should amputate that part of the body which is injurious to the body as a whole, if by removing it, the rest (of the body) can live in happiness.

devanagari: सर्वैरुपायैर्हन्तव्यः सम्भोजशयनासनैः । सुहृल्लिङ्गधरः शत्रुर्मुनेर्दुष्टमिवेन्द्रियम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvairupāyairhantavyaḥ sambhojaśayanāsanaiḥsuhṛlliṅgadharaḥ śatrurmunerduṣṭamivendriyam
translation:
Like an uncontrolled sense-organ, in the case of a sage, he is a veritable enemy masquerading as a friend (son.). He should be disposed of by all expedients, e.g. administering poison in food, killing him while sitting unguarded or while asleep.”
39-40 Commanded thus by their master, the demons with sharp teeth, terrific faces and reddish hair and beard, armed themselves with tridents, and giving out terrible yells and shouting “Gut him, kill him”, pierced Prahlāda in all the vital parts with their tridents while he was sitting quietly.

devanagari: नैरृतास्ते समादिष्टा भर्त्रा वै शूलपाणयः । तिग्मदंष्ट्रकरालास्यास्ताम्रश्मश्रुशिरोरुहाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nairṛtāste samādiṣṭā bhartrā vai śūlapāṇayaḥtigmadaṃṣṭrakarālāsyāstāmraśmaśruśiroruhāḥ

devanagari: नदन्तो भैरवं नादं छिन्धि भिन्धीति वादिनः । आसीनं चाहनन्शूलैः प्रह्रादं सर्वमर्मसु ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: nadanto bhairavaṃ nādaṃ chindhi bhindhīti vādinaḥāsīnaṃ cāhananśūlaiḥ prahrādaṃ sarvamarmasu

devanagari: परे ब्रह्मण्यनिर्देश्ये भगवत्यखिलात्मनि । युक्तात्मन्यफला आसन्नपुण्यस्येव सत्क्रियाः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: pare brahmaṇyanirdeśye bhagavatyakhilātmaniyuktātmanyaphalā āsannapuṇyasyeva satkriyāḥ
translation: All their attempts on (the life of) Prahlāda proved fruitless and ineffectual, like the pious acts of a person with no past meritorious deeds to his credit; for Prahlāda’s mind was in communion with the glorious Lord who is the Soul of all beings, the incomprehensible Supreme Brahman.

devanagari: प्रयासेऽपहते तस्मिन्दैत्येन्द्रः परिशङ्कितः । चकार तद्वधोपायान्निर्बन्धेन युधिष्ठिर ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: prayāse'pahate tasmindaityendraḥ pariśaṅkitaḥcakāra tadvadhopāyānnirbandhena yudhiṣṭhira
translation: When that attempt was thwarted, the king of demons grew alarmed and he persistently devised other expedients to kill Prahlāda, Oh Yudhiṣṭhira.

devanagari: दिग्गजैर्दन्दशूकेन्द्रैरभिचारावपातनैः । मायाभिः सन्निरोधैश्च गरदानैरभोजनैः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: diggajairdandaśūkendrairabhicārāvapātanaiḥmāyābhiḥ sannirodhaiśca garadānairabhojanaiḥ
translation: When the Asura could not kill his innocent son by employing elephants guarding the quarters, great serpents, by use of black magic, by hurling him down from mountain peaks, by using illusive powers (māyā), by confining him in dark holes (silos), administering him poison, by starvation, by exposing him to snow, winds, by throwing him in fire and by throwing mountains on him, he was plunged in the deepest anxiety and could not think of any other (effective) measure to kill him.

devanagari: हिमवाय्वग्निसलिलैः पर्वताक्रमणैरपि । न शशाक यदा हन्तुमपापमसुरः सुतम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: himavāyvagnisalilaiḥ parvatākramaṇairapina śaśāka yadā hantumapāpamasuraḥ sutam
translation: When the Asura could not kill his innocent son by employing elephants guarding the quarters, great serpents, by use of black magic, by hurling him down from mountain peaks, by using illusive powers (māyā), by confining him in dark holes (silos), administering him poison, by starvation, by exposing him to snow, winds, by throwing him in fire and by throwing mountains on him, he was plunged in the deepest anxiety and could not think of any other (effective) measure to kill him.

devanagari: चिन्तां दीर्घतमां प्राप्तस्तत्कर्तुं नाभ्यपद्यत । एष मे बह्वसाधूक्तो वधोपायाश्च निर्मिताः । तैस्तैद्रोहैरसद्धर्मैर्मुक्तः स्वेनैव तेजसा ...
english_devnagari: cintāṃ dīrghatamāṃ prāptastatkartuṃ nābhyapadyataeṣa me bahvasādhūkto vadhopāyāśca nirmitāḥtaistaidrohairasaddharmairmuktaḥ svenaiva tejasā
translation: (Hiraṇyakaśipu thought to himself). “This boy has been severely reproached by me now and again. Measures to kill him were also adopted. But by dint of his own power and energy, he has survived unaffected by those tortures and black spells.

devanagari: वर्तमानोऽविदूरे वै बालोऽप्यजडधीरयम् । न विस्मरति मेऽनार्यं शुनः शेप इव प्रभुः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: vartamāno'vidūre vai bālo'pyajaḍadhīrayamna vismarati me'nāryaṃ śunaḥ śepa iva prabhuḥ
translation: He is not away from my presence. Though he is still a boy, his mind is not daunted (through my fear). He is so powerful that he will not forget (and forgive) my unrighteousness (and maltreatment) like Śunaḥśepa.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
This refers to the legend of Śunaḥśepa in the Aitareya Brāhmaṇa. Śunaḥśepa disowned his father Ajīgarta for selling him for sacrifice to Hariścandra and adopted another—Viśvāmitra—gotra.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अप्रमेयानुभावोऽयमकुतश्चिद्भयोऽमरः । नूनमेतद्विरोधेन मृत्युर्मे भविता न वा ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: aprameyānubhāvo'yamakutaścidbhayo'maraḥnūnametadvirodhena mṛtyurme bhavitā na vā
translation: He is of immeasurable prowess; he entertains fear from nobody as if he is immortal. It is certain that my death will take place by confronting him or possibly it may not (overtake at all)

devanagari: इति तच्चिन्तया किञ्चिन्म्लानश्रियमधोमुखम् । शण्डामर्कावौशनसौ विविक्त इति होचतुः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti taccintayā kiñcinmlānaśriyamadhomukhamśaṇḍāmarkāvauśanasau vivikta iti hocatuḥ
translation: In this anxious mood, he was sitting with his countenance pale and downcast, when Śaṇḍa and Amarka, the two sons of Śukra spoke to him in private, as follows:

devanagari: जितं त्वयैकेन जगत्त्रयं भ्रुवोर्विजृम्भणत्रस्तसमस्तधिष्ण्यपम् । न तस्य चिन्त्यं तव नाथ चक्ष्वहे न वै शिशूनां गुणदोषयोः पदम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: jitaṃ tvayaikena jagattrayaṃ bhruvorvijṛmbhaṇatrastasamastadhiṣṇyapamna tasya cintyaṃ tava nātha cakṣvahe na vai śiśūnāṃ guṇadoṣayoḥ padam
translation: “You have single-handedly conquered the three worlds by terrifying all the Lokapālas (guardian deities of the world) by merely contracting your eye-brows. For such a (powerful) Lord as you are, we see no reason for anxiety. The behaviour of children should not be seriously judged as good or bad.

devanagari: इमं तु पाशैर्वरुणस्य बद्ध्वा निधेहि भीतो न पलायते यथा । बुद्धिश्च पुंसो वयसार्यसेवया यावद्गुरुर्भार्गव आगमिष्यति ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: imaṃ tu pāśairvaruṇasya baddhvā nidhehi bhīto na palāyate yathābuddhiśca puṃso vayasāryasevayā yāvadgururbhārgava āgamiṣyati
translation: Keep him bound down by the noose of Varuṇa so that he should not run away out of fear, till your teacher Śukra returns. A man’s understanding may improve with advancement of age and service of the noble ones.”

devanagari: तथेति गुरुपुत्रोक्तमनुज्ञायेदमब्रवीत् । धर्मो ह्यस्योपदेष्टव्यो राज्ञां यो गृहमेधिनाम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti guruputroktamanujñāyedamabravītdharmo hyasyopadeṣṭavyo rājñāṃ yo gṛhamedhinām
translation: Expressing his assent to the proposals of the preceptor’s sons, he ordered, “The duties prescribed for royal householders be taught to him.”

devanagari: धर्ममर्थं च कामं च नितरां चानुपूर्वशः । प्रह्रादायोचतू राजन्प्रश्रितावनताय च ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmamarthaṃ ca kāmaṃ ca nitarāṃ cānupūrvaśaḥprahrādāyocatū rājanpraśritāvanatāya ca
translation: Oh King! Then they taught in serial order the details only of Dharma, Artha, Kāma, to Prahlāda who was both modest and obedient.

devanagari: यथा त्रिवर्गं गुरुभिरात्मने उपशिक्षितम् । न साधु मेने तच्छिक्षां द्वन्द्वारामोपवर्णिताम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā trivargaṃ gurubhirātmane upaśikṣitamna sādhu mene tacchikṣāṃ dvandvārāmopavarṇitām
translation: (Though taught systematically), Prahlāda did not approve of the teaching of only the first three objects of human life (as mentioned above), as this instruction was meant for those who were interested in pairs like attachment and hatred, and in the enjoyment of objects of senses.

devanagari: यदाचार्यः परावृत्तो गृहमेधीयकर्मसु । वयस्यैर्बालकैस्तत्र सोपहूतः कृतक्षणैः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadācāryaḥ parāvṛtto gṛhamedhīyakarmasuvayasyairbālakaistatra sopahūtaḥ kṛtakṣaṇaiḥ
translation: When the teacher was not present (at his class) due to some household duties, he was invited by the school-companions of his age who found opportunity for play (and merrymaking).

devanagari: अथ तान्श्लक्ष्णया वाचा प्रत्याहूय महाबुधः । उवाच विद्वांस्तन्निष्ठां कृपया प्रहसन्निव ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tānślakṣṇayā vācā pratyāhūya mahābudhaḥuvāca vidvāṃstanniṣṭhāṃ kṛpayā prahasanniva
translation: Responding to their call in sweet words, the wise Prahlāda, knowing their attachment to him, spoke to them with a smile, out of grace.

devanagari: ते तु तद्गौरवात्सर्वे त्यक्तक्रीडापरिच्छदाः । बाला अदूषितधियो द्वन्द्वारामेरितेहितैः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: te tu tadgauravātsarve tyaktakrīḍāparicchadāḥbālā adūṣitadhiyo dvandvārāmeritehitaiḥ
translation: Out of great regard for him, all the boys whose minds were not vitiated by the advice or actions of persons (teachers) who were given to worldly enjoyments and were subject to love, hate etc., set aside all their play-things. With their eyes and hearts fixed on him, they sat round him. The Asura (Prahlāda) who was a great devotee of Viṣṇu and full of compassion and friendship, addressed them.

devanagari: पर्युपासत राजेन्द्र तन्न्यस्तहृदयेक्षणाः । तानाह करुणो मैत्रो महाभागवतोऽसुरः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: paryupāsata rājendra tannyastahṛdayekṣaṇāḥtānāha karuṇo maitro mahābhāgavato'suraḥ
translation:
Out of great regard for him, all the boys whose minds were not vitiated by the advice or actions of persons (teachers) who were given to worldly enjoyments and were subject to love, hate etc., set aside all their play-things. With their eyes and hearts fixed on him, they sat round him. The Asura (Prahlāda) who was a great devotee of Viṣṇu and full of compassion and friendship, addressed them.
[back to top]
